questions: difference between modern and classical architecture?
answers: Architecture is the art and science of designing buildings and structures. It combines creativity with engineering to create functional and beautiful spaces.
Architecture holo building ar structure design korar art. Architects plan kore kivabe ekta building dekhte hobe, function korbe, and safe thakbe.
An architect designs buildings, plans layouts, and ensures structures are safe and aesthetically pleasing. To become one, you typically need a degree in architecture and professional licensing.
Architecture covers everything from residential homes to skyscrapers. Famous examples include the Taj Mahal, Eiffel Tower, and Sydney Opera House. It blends art, math, and engineering together.
